message.content: {"schemaVersion": "0.1.0", "decisionId": "pilot-e1-036-turn-2-player-1", "agentId": "candidate-constrained-llm", "selectedActionId": "play-single-heart-4-copy1", "teamObjective": {"type": "suppress_opponent", "explanation": "Opponent 0 is down to 2 cards and has the lead. Playing the lowest legal winner reclaims the lead, denies the opponent a free continuation, and slows their path to finishing."}, "partnerBelief": {"summary": "No specific information about partner's hand; partner has 3 cards and has not acted yet.", "confidence": 0.3, "evidence": ["partner (player 3) has 3 cards remaining", "no prior plays from partner"]}, "opponentBelief": {"summary": "Leading opponent 0 has 2 cards left and just played a single 3 of hearts. They may still hold a heart or a trump, but no bombs or trump have been played.", "confidence": 0.4, "evidence": ["player 0 played a single 3 of hearts", "handCounts shows player 0 has 2 cards left", "playedCardSummary shows 0 trump, 0 bombs, 0 big cards played"]}, "actionRationale": {"primaryReason": "4 of hearts is the cheapest card that still beats the table lead. It wins the trick if no higher follow or trump appears, while preserving the 9 and 10 for later pressure.", "whyNotAlternatives": [{"actionId": "pass", "reason": "Passing lets opponent 0 keep the lead with only 2 cards remaining, risking they finish soon."}, {"actionId": "play-single-heart-9-copy1", "reason": "Wins the trick but uses a higher card unnecessarily when the 4 already beats the lead."}, {"actionId": "play-single-heart-10-copy1", "reason": "Same as the 9; overcommits a high card when the lowest winner is available."}]}, "riskAssessment": {"risks": ["Another player (including partner) may still beat the 4 of hearts.", "If opponent 0 holds a trump or a higher heart, the trick may still be lost or they may regain lead.", "Playing now uses one of only three cards and could leave few options later."], "mitigation": "By choosing the minimum winning card, resource consumption is minimized and stronger cards are retained to respond to future leads."}, "confidence": 0.85, "notes": "This is a straightforward follow decision: beat the lowest possible lead with the lowest possible winner."}

{"agentId": "candidate-constrained-llm", "decisionId": "pilot-e1-046-turn-2-player-1", "selectedActionId": "pass", "reasoning": {"summary": "Partner (player 0) led a single 3 of hearts. With opponents at 3 cards each and partner already down to 2 cards, the cooperative play is to let partner's lead stand rather than overtake it. Passing preserves my 4/9/10 for future support or my own finish, and avoids forcing my team to spend a beat unnecessarily.", "confidence": 0.85, "risk": "An opponent may beat the 3, but any of my cards would also only win this trick and then leave me leading with limited cards, which is not clearly better."}}
